description: Blog & Content Management - Build an MDX-powered blog with listing page, category filtering, individual post pages, SEO optimization, and RSS feed

userStories.title: MDX content processing setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need MDX processing configured so that blog posts and help articles can be authored as .mdx files.
acceptanceCriteria: Install and configure @next/mdx or contentlayer/velite for MDX processing
Content directory created at content/blog/ for blog MDX files

Frontmatter schema defined and typed via TypeScript interface: title (string), date (string ISO), author (string), category (string), excerpt (string), featuredImage (string path), tags (string array), featured (boolean, optional — marks post as pinned/featured on listing page)
Utility function getAllBlogPosts(): reads all blog posts, parses frontmatter, returns typed array sorted by date (newest first)
Utility function getBlogPostBySlug(slug): reads a single blog post by slug, returns typed post with content
Read time calculated from word count (~200 words per minute), returned as part of post metadata
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: MDX infrastructure is shared with PW-06 (help center articles). Set up the processing pipeline once, use it for both blog and help content.

userStories.title: Custom MDX components
userStories.description: As a content author, I want custom components available in MDX so that blog posts can include callouts, captioned images, and CTAs.
acceptanceCriteria: Callout component with variants: info (blue), tip (green), warning (yellow), important (red) — each with distinct icon and background color
ImageWithCaption component: Next.js Image with caption text below, responsive sizing, lazy loading for below-fold images
VideoEmbed component: responsive iframe wrapper for YouTube/Vimeo with 16:9 aspect ratio
CTABanner component: inline call-to-action with heading, text, and button linking to /signup
All components exported as mdx-components.tsx for MDX rendering
Components work in both light and dark mode
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: These MDX components are shared with PW-06 (help center). Additional help-specific components (StepByStep, UIPath) added in PW-06.

userStories.title: Sample blog posts
userStories.description: As a developer, I need at least two sample blog posts so that the blog pages can be developed and tested with real content.
acceptanceCriteria: Create content/blog/getting-started-with-vanman.mdx with full frontmatter, ~500 words of sample content in 'Product Updates' category
Post uses at least one custom MDX component (Callout and CTABanner)
Create content/blog/5-tips-for-moving-companies.mdx as a second sample post in 'Business Tips' category with ~500 words
Featured images: create placeholder images in public/blog/images/ (can be solid-color placeholders with text)
Both posts have realistic frontmatter (title, date, author, category, excerpt, featuredImage, tags)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Sample content should be realistic and relevant to the moving industry — not lorem ipsum.

userStories.title: Blog listing page
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to browse blog posts so that I can find articles relevant to my moving business.
acceptanceCriteria: Page at /blog
Grid of blog post cards: 2-col desktop, 1-col mobile
Each card shows: featured image thumbnail (16:9 aspect ratio), title, publication date, category tag (colored badge), 1-2 line excerpt, read time estimate
Posts ordered by publication date (newest first)
Featured/pinned post at top rendered as a larger hero-style card (post with featured: true in frontmatter, or most recent if none marked)
Pagination: 10 posts per page with Next/Previous page links (SSG pages for /blog, /blog/page/2, etc.)
Page statically generated
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Blog listing is the main entry point for content marketing. Featured post at top gives editorial control over what visitors see first.

userStories.title: Blog category filtering
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to filter blog posts by category so that I can find content on a specific topic.
acceptanceCriteria: Category filter bar at top of blog listing: All, Business Tips, Industry News, Product Updates, Guides & How-tos, Customer Stories
Clicking a category filters the post list to show only posts in that category
URL updates to /blog/category/[slug] for shareability and SEO (statically generated per category)
Active category visually highlighted in the filter bar
Category pages show post count in heading (e.g. 'Business Tips (12 articles)')
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Category pages are SSG for SEO. Categories should match the content strategy: Business Tips for organic search, Product Updates for existing users, Customer Stories for social proof.

userStories.title: Blog post page — layout and content rendering
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to read a full blog post with proper formatting.
acceptanceCriteria: Page at /blog/[slug] — statically generated from MDX files
Post header: title (h1), publication date formatted nicely, author name, read time estimate, category tag linked to category filter page
Featured image rendered full-width or hero below header using Next.js Image with eager loading
Article body rendered from MDX with rich formatting: headings (h2-h4), ordered/unordered lists, images, blockquotes, tables, code blocks with syntax highlighting
Comfortable reading width (max-w-prose ~65ch), generous line height (leading-relaxed)
Author bio card at bottom with name and short bio text
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Reading experience is critical for time-on-site and engagement. max-w-prose (~65ch) is optimal for readability. Featured image should be eager-loaded; inline images lazy-loaded.

userStories.title: Blog post page — TOC, sharing, and related posts
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want a table of contents, share buttons, and related posts so that I can navigate and discover more content.
acceptanceCriteria: Table of contents sidebar: auto-generated from h2/h3 headings in the MDX content, sticky on desktop, collapsed dropdown on mobile
Active heading highlighted in TOC as user scrolls (intersection observer)
Share buttons: copy link (with copied confirmation), X/Twitter share, LinkedIn share, Facebook share
CTA banner after the post body: 'Try VanMan free for 14 days' with signup button linking to /signup
Related posts section: 3 post cards from the same category (or matching tags if insufficient)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: TOC with active highlighting improves UX on long posts. Share buttons drive organic referral traffic. Related posts reduce bounce rate by guiding visitors to more content.

userStories.title: Blog SEO metadata and structured data
userStories.description: As the marketing team, I want each blog post to have proper SEO metadata so that posts rank well.
acceptanceCriteria: generateMetadata on blog post pages returns: title (with '| VanMan Blog' suffix), description (from excerpt), canonical URL
Open Graph tags: og:title, og:description, og:image (featured image), og:type=article, og:article:published_time, og:article:author
Twitter Card tags: twitter:card=summary_large_image, twitter:title, twitter:description, twitter:image
JSON-LD Article structured data: headline, datePublished, dateModified, author (Person schema), image, publisher (Organization schema)
Blog listing page also has proper meta tags
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Uses the SEO helper from PW-08 if available, otherwise implements locally.

userStories.title: RSS feed and sitemap inclusion
userStories.description: As a subscriber, I want an RSS feed so that I can follow VanMan's blog in my feed reader.
acceptanceCriteria: RSS feed generated at /blog/rss.xml via a route handler (app/blog/rss.xml/route.ts)
Feed includes for each post: title, description (excerpt), link (full URL), publication date, author name
Feed metadata: channel title 'VanMan Blog', description, link to /blog
RSS autodiscovery link tag added to <head> of all blog pages: <link rel='alternate' type='application/rss+xml'>
Feed validates as valid RSS 2.0 XML
All blog posts included in the site sitemap with lastmod dates
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Blog search
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to search blog posts so that I can quickly find articles on a specific topic.
acceptanceCriteria: Search bar on the blog listing page (/blog)
Client-side search across post titles, excerpts, and tags using a pre-built search index
Search index generated at build time as a static JSON file
Instant results as user types (debounced at ~300ms)
Results show: post title, category, excerpt with highlighted matching text
No results state with friendly message
New posts automatically included in search index on next build/deploy (no manual step beyond merging the MDX file)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Search index can be shared with PW-06 help center search (same library — Fuse.js or FlexSearch).
